title: Kalimeris shimadae
intro: Kalimeris shimadae, also known as Japanese aster, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. The plant has been traditionally used in East Asian medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Kalimeris shimadae include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Kalimeris shimadae is often prepared as a decoction or dried extract, and can be found in various herbal preparations, including teas and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.

It belongs to the genus Kalimeris, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to East Asia. Kalimeris shimadae is further classified as a perennial herb, characterized by its attractive purple flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is native to the mountainous regions of Japan and is often found growing in rocky, well-drained areas. Kalimeris shimadae is an important part of the local ecosystem, providing a source of food and shelter for various insects and small animals.
